name: models
context: OpenAI model selection guide — GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, o1, o3, embeddings, and DALL-E
info: OpenAI offers multiple model families optimized for different tasks. GPT-4o is the flagship multimodal model for general-purpose text and vision tasks. GPT-4o-mini is optimized for speed and cost on simpler tasks. o1 and o3 are reasoning models that use chain-of-thought internally before answering, excelling at complex math, code, and analysis. text-embedding-3-small and text-embedding-3-large produce vector embeddings for search and RAG (see [[openai/embeddings]]). DALL-E 3 generates images from text prompts (see [[openai/images]]). This guide compares capabilities, pricing, context windows, and best-use scenarios to help select the right model for each task.
triggers: which openai model should i use
body:
# Model Selection Guide

OpenAI provides several model families, each optimized for different workloads. This guide helps you select the right model based on task complexity, latency requirements, and budget.

## Model Comparison

| Model | Type | Context Window | Input Price | Output Price | Best For |
|-------|------|---------------|-------------|--------------|----------|
| GPT-4o | Chat | 128k tokens | $2.50/1M | $10.00/1M | General-purpose, multimodal, coding |
| GPT-4o-mini | Chat | 128k tokens | $0.15/1M | $0.60/1M | Fast tasks, high volume, cost-sensitive |
| o1 | Reasoning | 200k tokens | $15.00/1M | $60.00/1M | Complex reasoning, math, analysis |
| o3 | Reasoning | 200k tokens | $10.00/1M | $40.00/1M | Advanced reasoning, code, research |
| o3-mini | Reasoning | 200k tokens | $1.10/1M | $4.40/1M | Cost-effective reasoning |
| text-embedding-3-small | Embedding | 8,191 tokens | $0.02/1M | N/A | High-volume search, RAG |
| text-embedding-3-large | Embedding | 8,191 tokens | $0.13/1M | N/A | Maximum retrieval accuracy |
| DALL-E 3 | Image | N/A | $0.04-0.12/image | N/A | Image generation |

*Prices are approximate and subject to change. Check [platform.openai.com/pricing](https://platform.openai.com/pricing) for current rates.*

## Pricing Considerations

Costs are measured in tokens (roughly 4 characters per token for English text):

- **Input tokens** — your prompt, system message, and conversation history
- **Output tokens** — the model's response (usually more expensive)
- **Reasoning tokens** — o1/o3 models also charge for internal chain-of-thought tokens

Strategies to reduce cost:
- Use GPT-4o-mini for simple tasks (classification, extraction, summarization)
- Use GPT-4o for complex tasks requiring nuance and accuracy
- Use o1/o3 only when complex reasoning is essential
- Keep system prompts concise to reduce input tokens
- Set `max_tokens` to limit output length
- Use [[openai/embeddings]] with smaller dimensions to reduce vector storage costs

## Context Windows

| Model | Context Window | Effective Long-Context Use |
|-------|---------------|---------------------------|
| GPT-4o | 128k tokens | ~300 pages of text |
| GPT-4o-mini | 128k tokens | ~300 pages of text |
| o1 | 200k tokens | ~500 pages of text |
| o3 | 200k tokens | ~500 pages of text |

Larger context windows enable:
- Analyzing long documents without chunking
- Multi-file code review
- Extended conversation history
- RAG with more retrieved context (see [[openai/embeddings#use-cases]])

## Best Practices

### Model Selection by Task

| Task | Recommended Model | Reason |
|------|------------------|--------|
| General Q&A | GPT-4o-mini | Fast, cheap, sufficient quality |
| Code generation | GPT-4o | Strong coding, good accuracy |
| Complex reasoning | o1 or o3 | Chain-of-thought reasoning |
| Data extraction | GPT-4o-mini + structured output | Fast, schema-constrained (see [[openai/chat#structured-output]]) |
| Document analysis | GPT-4o | Long context, multimodal |
| Image understanding | GPT-4o | Vision capabilities (see [[openai/chat#vision]]) |
| Semantic search | text-embedding-3-small | Cost-effective embeddings (see [[openai/embeddings]]) |
| High-accuracy RAG | text-embedding-3-large | Best retrieval quality (see [[openai/embeddings#models]]) |
| Image generation | DALL-E 3 | High-quality images (see [[openai/images]]) |
| Agent with tools | GPT-4o | Reliable function calling (see [[openai/function-calling]]) |
| Persistent assistant | GPT-4o | Full assistant features (see [[openai/assistants]]) |

### General Guidelines

- Start with GPT-4o-mini, upgrade to GPT-4o if quality is insufficient
- Use structured output (see [[openai/chat#structured-output]]) for reliable data extraction
- Set temperature=0 for deterministic outputs, higher for creative tasks
- Monitor token usage via the `usage` field in API responses
- Implement retry with exponential backoff for rate limit errors (see [[openai#rate-limits]])

sections.title: Model comparison
sections.body: GPT-4o: flagship multimodal (text + vision), 128k context, best general-purpose quality. GPT-4o-mini: fast and cheap, 128k context, ideal for high-volume simple tasks. o1: reasoning model, 200k context, excels at math, science, complex analysis. o3: advanced reasoning and code, 200k context. o3-mini: cost-effective reasoning. text-embedding-3-small/large: vector embeddings (see [[openai/embeddings]]). DALL-E 3: image generation (see [[openai/images]]).

sections.title: Pricing
sections.body: Costs are per million tokens. GPT-4o-mini is ~17x cheaper than GPT-4o for input. o1/o3 reasoning models also charge for internal chain-of-thought tokens. Embedding models charge input only. DALL-E charges per image. Use GPT-4o-mini for volume, GPT-4o for quality, o1/o3 for complex reasoning. Check platform.openai.com/pricing for current rates.

sections.title: Context windows
sections.body: GPT-4o and GPT-4o-mini: 128k tokens (~300 pages). o1 and o3: 200k tokens (~500 pages). Embedding models: 8,191 tokens per input. Larger context enables long document analysis, extended conversations, and more RAG context. Token count includes system prompt, conversation history, and tools.

sections.title: Best practices
sections.body: Start with GPT-4o-mini, upgrade if needed. Use structured output for data extraction (see [[openai/chat#structured-output]]). Set temperature=0 for deterministic results. Monitor `usage` in responses. Implement retry with backoff (see [[openai#rate-limits]]). Use [[openai/function-calling]] for tool integration. Choose embedding dimensions based on quality/storage tradeoffs (see [[openai/embeddings#dimensions]]).
